economic_notes: First Nation operates multiple subsidiaries including bulk fuels, construction, management, insurance and numbered companies. Post year-end purchased property in Winnipeg with FNFA interim financing of $4,053,000. Completed Phase 14 and Phase 15 housing construction subsequent to year-end.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to determine Asset Retirement Obligations (PS 3280) as First Nation identified no ARO liabilities. Fiscal year is March 31, 2023 but auditor report is dated January 30, 2025 — very late filing. Seven subsidiaries fully consolidated. Large increase in deferred revenue from $3M to $16M primarily due to ISC capital and program funding. Significant deferred revenue for Child and Family Services Apprehension Prevention ($4.16M) and Wastewater Plant Upgrade ($4.4M). OSR calculation results in negative as government transfers exceed total revenue due to large deferred revenue adjustment reducing recognized revenue. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($92,996,447) + CMHC subsidies ($1,559,603) + Province of Manitoba ($1,755,832) = $96,311,882. Annual debt service calculated as FNFA repayments ($637,091) + CMHC repayments ($423,331) + other long-term debt ($286,606) = $1,347,028. Community Service and Development expenses used for infrastructure proxy; Housing not broken out separately.
